title: The boy who counts crows
summary: Every morning, a small boy counts the crows on his way to school. His brother asks why.
body:
Bao is seven years old. Every morning, he walks to school with his big brother, Minh. Bao always looks at the trees.

'Why do you look at the trees?' Minh asks.
'I count the crows,' Bao says.

On Monday, Bao counts six crows. On Tuesday, he counts four. On Wednesday, he counts ten. On Thursday, he counts only two. On Friday, he counts seven.

Minh does not understand. 'Why do you count crows? Crows are not important.'

Bao says, 'They are important to me.'

One day, Bao is sick. He stays at home. Minh walks to school alone. He looks at the trees. He does not want to look. But he looks.

He sees a crow. Then another. Then five more. Minh starts to count.

At school, Minh tells his teacher. 'Today I saw eight crows on the way to school.' His teacher is surprised. She says, 'I have walked this street for twenty years. I have never seen one crow.'

Minh thinks about this all day. When he comes home, Bao is in bed.

'Bao,' Minh says, 'how do you see the crows? Other people walk on this street, but they do not see them.'

Bao smiles. 'I look up,' he says. 'Most people look down.'

annotations.meaningVi: ngẩng đầu lên / nhìn lên trên

grammarNotes.title: Simple present for routines
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng hiện tại đơn để nói về việc xảy ra hàng ngày hoặc thường xuyên. Với chủ ngữ số ít (he, she, it), động từ thêm -s: 'he walks', 'he looks', 'he counts'. Trong bài: 'Every morning, he walks to school.' 'Bao always looks at the trees.'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: The simple present describes daily routines. With he/she/it, add -s to the verb: 'he walks', 'he looks', 'he counts'. From the story: 'Every morning, he walks to school.' 'Bao always looks at the trees.'

translationVi:
Bảo bảy tuổi. Sáng nào em cũng đi bộ đến trường với anh trai, Minh. Bảo luôn nhìn lên cây.

'Sao em cứ nhìn lên cây?' Minh hỏi.
'Em đếm quạ,' Bảo nói.

Thứ Hai, Bảo đếm được sáu con quạ. Thứ Ba, em đếm được bốn. Thứ Tư, em đếm được mười. Thứ Năm, em chỉ đếm được hai. Thứ Sáu, em đếm được bảy.

Minh không hiểu. 'Đếm quạ làm gì? Quạ có quan trọng đâu.'

Bảo nói: 'Quan trọng với em mà.'

Một hôm, Bảo bị ốm. Em ở nhà. Minh đi đến trường một mình. Anh nhìn lên cây. Anh không muốn nhìn. Nhưng anh vẫn nhìn.

Anh thấy một con quạ. Rồi một con nữa. Rồi năm con nữa. Minh bắt đầu đếm.

Đến trường, Minh kể với cô giáo. 'Hôm nay em thấy tám con quạ trên đường đến trường.' Cô giáo ngạc nhiên. Cô nói: 'Cô đi con đường này hai mươi năm rồi. Cô chưa từng thấy một con quạ nào cả.'

Minh suy nghĩ về chuyện đó cả ngày. Khi anh về nhà, Bảo đang nằm trên giường.

'Bảo,' Minh nói, 'sao em thấy được lũ quạ? Người ta cũng đi con đường này, nhưng không ai thấy chúng cả.'

Bảo cười. 'Em nhìn lên,' em nói. 'Phần lớn người ta cứ nhìn xuống.'

questions.prompt: Who walks to school with Bao?
questions.explanation: 'Every morning, he walks to school with his big brother, Minh.'

questions.prompt: What does Bao look at every morning?
How many crows does Bao count on Wednesday?
What does Minh think about counting crows?
options: He thinks crows are not important.
He thinks Bao should count cars.
He thinks the teacher will be angry.
questions.explanation: 'Why do you count crows? Crows are not important.'

questions.prompt: Why does Minh walk to school alone one day?
options: Bao is sick and stays home.
questions.explanation: 'One day, Bao is sick. He stays at home. Minh walks to school alone.'

questions.prompt: What does Minh's teacher say when he tells her about the crows?
options: She has walked the street for twenty years and never seen a crow.
She has counted the same crows.
She tells him to stop looking up.
questions.explanation: 'I have walked this street for twenty years. I have never seen one crow.'

questions.prompt: Why does Bao see crows when other people do not?
options: He looks up; most people look down.
He goes earlier in the morning.
questions.explanation: 'I look up. Most people look down.'

questions.prompt: How does Minh change in the story?
options: He starts to look up and count the crows himself.
He starts to walk a different way to school.
He moves to a new city.
questions.explanation: 'He looks at the trees… Minh starts to count.'
questions.hint: What does Minh do on the day Bao is sick?

questions.prompt: What is the main idea of the story?
options: What you see depends on where you decide to look.
Crows are common in this city.
Children should go to school every day.
questions.explanation: Bao sees crows because he looks up; Minh and the teacher do not, because they don't.

blanks.explanation: Bao always looks at the trees.
Minh says crows are not important.
Bao is sick and stays at home.
Minh walks to school alone that day.
He tells his teacher about the crows.
Bao looks up, where the crows are.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Bao sees crows because he looks up. The teacher has walked the same street for twenty years without seeing any. What is something you walk past every day but probably do not really see — and what would you have to do to start seeing it?
